body.title: Rack late-beta certification test suite — all components except Igor
body.result: fixed 30 pre-existing test failures (proof fields, GrannyShim health_surface, env var conftest fix); full suite validation deferred to overnight run
body.description:
Comprehensive test coverage certifying the rack is late-beta quality. If someone starts building on this, they hit one or two rough edges but mostly everything works as expected. Excludes Igor cognition (separate eval story).

Three tiers:
1. Unit tests — each device in isolation: postgres, inference, web_server, sensor, queue, granny, librarian, scraps, workspace, installer
2. Integration tests — device-to-device over real bus: announce → manifest roundtrip; queue ticket files → Granny routes → cc_task_listener dispatches; Librarian recall returns seeded memory; scraps embed() returns correct-dimension vector
3. Contract tests — every device class implementing BaseDevice passes start/stop/health/self_test contract checks
4. End-to-end — full rack boot: skeleton starts, device registers, agent announces, receives manifest with tools, calls a tool, gets result

devices/rack_test/ is the test fixture seed. Expand it.

**Affected files:** devices/rack_test/, tests/ (all device subdirs), pytest configuration
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; tests hit real Postgres; no mocks for infrastructure components
**Scope boundary:** IN — all rack devices except devices/igor/; contract test harness in rack_test; OUT — Igor cognition, pe_chain, NE, TWM
**Completion criteria:** pytest tests/ (excluding tests/igor/) all green; coverage report shows >80% line coverage on each non-Igor device; a new developer can run 'pytest tests/' on a fresh checkout and see green
